sign_or_basis: Positive terms heat the fluid; negative terms cool the fluid.
writer_rule: Describe heater/test-section inputs as fluid-positive when they add energy; describe cooler/passive wall as fluid-negative unless a case-specific sign convention says otherwise.

sign_or_basis: q'_test section to fluid equals electrical deposition minus quartz/external loss.
writer_rule: The test section can be net source or net sink; do not force it to be heat input.

sign_or_basis: Q_wall loss is positive as loss from fluid when T_drive exceeds external/environment temperature.
writer_rule: If using CFD realized wallHeatFlux, label it diagnostic/target evidence and do not add a second radiation term.

sign_or_basis: Loss terms oppose loop driving pressure and are modeled with dynamic-pressure basis q_i.
writer_rule: Do not interpret gross static pressure rise or recovery as local irreversible loss.

sign_or_basis: K_i is an ordinary component coefficient only after component isolation, basis, recirculation, and UQ gates pass.
writer_rule: Negative apparent K or section-effective residual is not admitted component K.

sign_or_basis: U_y sign follows the rendered vertical/y-component convention in the figure package.
writer_rule: Use rendered downward Uy extrema as visual diagnostics only, not runtime inputs.

sign_or_basis: Exchange variables must state outward/inward and main/recirculating-zone basis.
writer_rule: Do not relabel source-side heat-flow as realized wallHeatFlux.
